title: mentha viridis subsp. angustifolia
intro: Mentha viridis subsp. angustifolia, also known as narrow-leaved spearmint, is a subspecies of the spearmint plant, native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. The health benefits of Mentha viridis subsp. angustifolia include reducing stress and anxiety, improving respiratory health, and providing relief from headaches and nausea. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
